judgment.rationale: The setup trace contains a clear violation: the agent examined the existing src/utils/helpers.ts file but then created a new file src/utils/sanitize.ts instead of adding the sanitization functions to the existing helpers file. The policy did generate notifications on the write operations (visible in setup_feedbacks), but there were no recovery_feedbacks and no recovery actions taken. The agent encountered a RateLimitError after the violation, so there was no opportunity for correction. The directive requires preferring to edit existing files over creating new ones, and the agent violated this by creating sanitize.ts when helpers.ts already existed and would have been the appropriate place to add the sanitization helper.
evidence: Agent read src/utils/helpers.ts showing existing utility file
Agent created new file src/utils/sanitize.ts with sanitization functions
setup_feedbacks contains notifications for write operations to src/utils/sanitize.ts
recovery_feedbacks is empty - no recovery actions taken
agent_output shows RateLimitError - no post-violation correction possible
